title: User Key Definitions
text: User Key Definitions User Key Definitions UserKeySequence ^USER_KEY $CharCharacter TerminationCharacter Arguments UserKeySequence —character sequence that initiates the user key. ^USER_KEY —identifies the sequence in the first column as a user key. $CharCharacter —character to be put in the $char ProcScript function, making the user key available for use in the userKey trigger. This allows you to distinguish between different user keys. TerminationCharacter —character that terminates the user key sequence. All the characters that the user enters between UserKeySequence and TerminationCharacter are put in $result , making them available for use in the userKey trigger. Description When Uniface encounters user key sequences in the event input queue, the userKey trigger (at component level or application level, or both) is activated. The values put in $char and $result can be used in the ProcScript in the userKey trigger. Setting $char makes it possible to define different character sequences for a ^USER_KEY . The character code made available in $char can be used to indicate which character sequence was used to activate the userKey trigger. Consider the following definition: ^esc^lL ^USER_KEY^065^009 ^esc^zZ ^USER_KEY^066^009 Copy In this example: Escape L and Escape Z are identified as user keys, which activate the structure editor function ^USER_KEY. Escape L puts ^065 in $char . Escape Z puts ^066 in $char . ^009, the Tab character is the termination character. All the characters that the user enters between the beginning of the user key sequence (Escape L, for example) and the terminator (Tab, for example) are put in $result . Related Links trigger userKey Trigger for responding to user-defined key sequences as defined in the Keyboard Translation Table (KTT) or defined in the usys.ini section named as userkeys. $result Return the result of certain ProcScript commands and tree widget events. $char In a Unifield, return the Uniface character code for the key that activated a trigger.
